cards.name: FFWT-HAC-CVCD signal invariants
cards.statement: A multi-scale fractal wavelet pipeline enriched with algebra-aware covariance, correlation and coherence features may improve anomaly detection, classification, denoising or interpretability on structured signals compared with classical baselines.
assumptions: Multi-scale structure is present in the data.
Algebraic features encode measurable structure rather than decorative complexity.
A real-valued robust projection is preserved.
predictions: The pipeline should beat at least one baseline on at least one relevant metric.
Ablations should clarify which algebraic features are useful.

tests.name: synthetic_multiscale_classification
tests.success_condition: beats at least one baseline with controlled cost
tests.falsifier: no improvement on multiple datasets after ablation

positive_memory: FFWT-HAC-CVCD is a priority because it is testable with code and datasets.
negative_memory: Do not treat exotic algebra as useful without a metric gain or interpretable invariant.
residues: Which tasks truly benefit from quaternionic/octonionic features remains unknown.
next_actions: Implement a minimal benchmark comparing FFWT-HAC-CVCD to FFT/wavelet/PCA baselines.

cards.name: Fractal RLC resonance laboratory
cards.statement: Conductive fractal graph geometries can produce distinctive multi-scale resonance spectra, localized modes or band-gap-like signatures in LC/RLC network simulations.
assumptions: The graph topology meaningfully changes the circuit Laplacian or admittance matrix.
Comparisons are made against regular and random graph baselines.
predictions: Fractal graphs should exhibit recognizable spectral signatures.
Some modes may be localized or robust to limited defects.

tests.name: graph_spectral_response
tests.metric: eigenfrequency distribution, IPR localization, frequency response peaks
tests.success_condition: fractal graph has repeatable distinguishable spectral features
tests.falsifier: signatures vanish under baseline comparison or small perturbations

positive_memory: This branch is physically simulable before any material claim is made.
negative_memory: Resonant/topological signatures do not imply superconductivity.
A superconductivity claim requires resistance, Meissner, gap and phase-coherence evidence.
residues: Which graph invariants best predict useful response remains open.
next_actions: Build fractal_rlc_lab and compare Sierpinski, triadic and baseline graphs.

cards.name: Bayes-Tristan hypothesis prioritization
cards.statement: A tensor-valued probability vector can help prioritize hypotheses by separating truth, usefulness, fertility, testability, safety, compressibility, novelty and value.
assumptions: Explicit multi-axis scoring reduces confusion between proof and fertility.
The scoring rule is transparent enough to be audited.
predictions: Hypothesis portfolios should become easier to triage.
OAK next-actions should become more concrete.

tests.name: twenty_card_prioritization
tests.metric: clarity of ranking, number of actionable next tests, duplicate-error reduction
tests.success_condition: produces a defensible top-10 roadmap from at least 20 cards
tests.falsifier: ranking is arbitrary or does not improve actionability

positive_memory: Separating truth from fertility is central to the Tristan scientific hygiene layer.
negative_memory: Do not promote high-fertility speculation as high-truth evidence.
next_actions: Implement bayes_tristan_engine.py with transparent weights and JSON/YAML ingestion.
